name: RRUFF Database - Preprocess Spectra
description: Comprehensive resource of raw Raman spectra for over 1,000 mineral species, representing a diverse array of crystallographic structures and chemical compositions measured under varying experimental conditions (e.g., 532 nm and 785 nm).
citeAs: @inbook{Lafuente_2015, title={The power of databases: The RRUFF project}, ISBN={9783110417104}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9783110417104-003}, DOI={10.1515/9783110417104-003}, booktitle={Highlights in Mineralogical Crystallography}, publisher={DE GRUYTER}, author={Lafuente, Barbara and Downs, R. T. and Yang, H. and Stone, N.}, year={2015}, month=nov, pages={1--30}}
citation: Lafuente, B., Downs, R. T., Yang, H., & Stone, N. (2015). The power of databases: the RRUFF project. Highlights in Mineralogical Crystallography, T Armbruster and R M Danisi, Eds., Berlin, Germany, W. De Gruyter, 1–30.

distribution.name: RRUFF Database - Preprocess Spectra
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Class label: string identifier of the sample class.

rai:dataCollection: Raman spectra were acquired experimentally using a laser spectrometer. Each spectrum represents the scattered light intensity as a function of Raman shift (cm⁻¹).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods. Labels were assigned by domain experts or clinical diagnosis; no crowdsourced annotation was used.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical, biological, or material samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Data were acquired under controlled laboratory conditions and may not generalise to field or clinical settings. Spectral profiles are influenced by instrument-specific factors (laser wavelength, detector type, optical path length, integration time). Class imbalances may be present in classification datasets.
rai:dataLimitations: Reference spectra represent ideal laboratory conditions; real-world samples may show additional interference from impurities, fluorescence, or surface effects. Note: No explicit license was stated by the original data providers; CC BY 4.0 has been assumed. Users should verify the applicable terms with the original authors.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Facilitates non-destructive material characterisation and identification with applications in geology, environmental science, art conservation, and industrial quality assurance.
rai:dataUseCases: Spectral library matching, material identification, disease screening, compound recognition, quality control. Not recommended for direct clinical diagnosis without professional validation and regulatory approval.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on solid or liquid material samples including minerals, polymers, and organic pigments. Preprocessing: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication. Annotation: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods.
